name: Chocolate Babka
recipeIngredient: 5 tablespoons butter or margarine (+extra for greasing)
1/2 cup finely chopped walnuts or pecans
recipeInstructions:
1. Place milk in a small saucepan and heat it just to the point where it is ready to boil. Remove from heat, add butter or margarine, and cool for 10 minutes in the refrigerator.
2. Generously grease the bottom, middle, and sides of a standard-sized tube or bundt pan.
3. Place water in a large bowl and sprinkle yeast evenly over it.
4. Add the milk mixture to the yeast when the milk mixture is definitely cool to wrist temperature; then add sugar and salt.
5. Add flour one cup at a time, mixing after each addition.
6. After all the flour is mixed in, knead for 5 to 10 minutes on a floured surface. Add small amounts of flour if necessary to keep the dough from getting too sticky.
7. Clean and grease the bowl. Add kneaded dough and oil or butter the top surface. Place it in a warm place to rise until doubled; this will take approximately 2 hours.
8. Grind chocolate chips in a food processor with steel blade or blender until they resemble coarse meal. Transfer to a small bowl and combine with cocoa. Sprinkle 1/3 cup of this mixture into the bottom of the greased pan, then sprinkle chopped nuts evenly over it.
9. When dough has doubled in bulk, punch down and knead for 5 to 10 minutes on a floured surface.
10. Roll dough into a large oval with rolling pin: 9-10 inches wide at the middle and 16-17 inches long.
11. Leaving a 1/2 inch rim around the outer edge, sprinkle remaining chocolate filling evenly over the dough. Roll it tightly along the long edge, pinching edges to seal them.
12. Carefully lift babka and ease into pan, making as even a circle as possible. Pat firmly into place and seal the two ends with a bit of water and a good pinch.
13. Let rise at room temperature 45 minutes, or refrigerate in an airtight plastic bag for up to two days until ready to bake.
14. Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit. Bake 45 to 50 minutes, or until hollow when thumped.
15. Remove chocolate babka from pan and invert onto plate, so chocolate-nut coating is on top. Cool at least 30 minutes.
